title: Qix Clone (pygame renderer)
pitch: A single-arena Qix clone where the real design fix is a Pressure Meter that punishes border-turtling: read the board, commit to a cut into open space, race to close it before the Qix or a spawned Sparx catches you, bank the claimed area, and repeat until 75% of the board is yours.
core_loop: Read the board: observe Qix position/trajectory, current claimed %, and Pressure Meter fill while standing on the border
Commit: press a direction off the border into open space, entering the vulnerable 'drawing' state (no idle-while-drawing allowed)
Execute: reach another claimed/border edge to close the shape; holding Shift draws slower for a higher score multiplier but extends exposure time
Resolve: flood fill splits the enclosed region, the side without the Qix is claimed, score/combo updates, Qix speed escalates with claimed %, Pressure Meter drains
Repeat commit-execute-resolve until claimed area reaches 75% (victory) or lives reach 0 (game over)
controls: Arrow keys / WASD: 4-directional movement, snapped to grid axes (no diagonals)
Hold Shift while moving: slow draw (2x point multiplier, half draw speed); release for fast draw (1x multiplier, full speed)
Enter / Space: confirm on menu, game-over, and victory screens
quality_bar: Movement and all entity motion use delta-time (position += direction * speed * dt) so behavior is identical at 30fps and 240fps
Flood fill correctly splits enclosed regions and claims only the side not containing the Qix, verified by a standalone prototype/test before UI polish
Zero circular imports: dependency direction strictly core -> utils -> entities -> world -> engine -> states -> main, enforced by never importing states/entities/world from engine
Pressure Meter measurably prevents indefinite border-turtling: sustained idling on the border provably increases Sparx count or Qix speed within a bounded time window
All state transitions (MENU/PLAYING/PAUSED/GAME_OVER/VICTORY) are explicit via StateManager.switch_to, with no state mutating another state's internals directly
Game runs at a stable frame rate with no unhandled exceptions across a full menu -> play -> victory/game-over -> menu cycle

files.name: main.py
files.purpose: Single entry point: initializes pygame, builds screen/clock/AssetManager/Renderer/InputHandler, constructs GameContext, registers all States with StateManager, and runs App.run(). Contains def main() -> None guarded by if __name__ == '__main__'.

files.name: game/core/constants.py
files.purpose: All tunable numeric constants (screen size, grid cell size, speeds, claim target, starting lives, Pressure Meter fill/drain rates, escalation thresholds). No logic, no imports from elsewhere in game/.

files.name: game/core/types.py
files.purpose: Shared value types and enums used across every layer: Vec2 math, CellState, GameState, InputIntent. Zero dependencies on other game/ packages.

files.name: game/core/context.py
files.purpose: GameContext dataclass threading engine services (screen, renderer, assets) and mutable run state (dt, score, lives, level, pressure) through every state/entity update call each frame.

files.name: game/utils/geometry.py
files.purpose: Pure geometry helpers: segment intersection tests, point-in-cell, grid/pixel conversions used by Trail self-intersection checks and collision tests.
public_api: def segments_intersect(p1: Vec2, p2: Vec2, p3: Vec2, p4: Vec2) -> bool
def point_to_cell(p: Vec2, cell_size: int) -> tuple[int, int]
def cell_to_point(cx: int, cy: int, cell_size: int) -> Vec2

files.name: game/engine/input.py
files.purpose: Translates raw pygame events and key state into a single frame's InputIntent. No knowledge of states or entities.

files.name: game/engine/renderer.py
files.purpose: Thin wrapper over pygame.draw and surface blits; the only module that issues raw pygame drawing calls, keeping draw calls out of entity/state logic bodies.
public_api: Renderer.line(p1: Vec2, p2: Vec2, color, width: int) -> None
Renderer.polyline(points: list[Vec2], color, width: int) -> None
Renderer.circle(center: Vec2, radius: float, color, width: int) -> None
Renderer.text(text: str, pos: Vec2, font, color) -> None

files.name: game/engine/app.py
files.purpose: Owns the fixed top-level loop: tick clock for dt, poll input, delegate to StateManager for input/update/draw, flip display, quit cleanly. Never imports states/entities/world.

files.name: game/states/state_manager.py
files.purpose: Registers concrete State instances per GameState id and dispatches handle_input/update/draw to whichever is current; owns explicit transition table logic via switch_to.

files.name: game/world/board.py
files.purpose: Combines Grid, Trail, and flood-fill area resolution into the single board API states/entities use: border/claim queries, cut lifecycle, percent claimed, border polyline for Sparx patrol and rendering.
public_api: Board.finish_cut(end_cell: tuple[int, int], qix_cell: tuple[int, int]) -> int
def flood_fill_region(grid: Grid, seed: tuple[int, int], blocked: frozenset) -> set
def resolve_cut(grid: Grid, cut_cells: list, qix_cell: tuple[int, int]) -> int

files.name: game/entities/entities.py
files.purpose: Player, Qix, and Sparx entity classes sharing the Entity ABC; all movement is delta-time based (position += direction * speed * dt). Depends on core/utils/world types only, never on states.

files.name: game/states/game_states.py
files.purpose: Concrete State implementations: MenuState, PlayingState, PausedState, GameOverState, VictoryState. PlayingState wires Board + Player + Qix + Sparx + Pressure Meter and drives collisions/escalation/win-loss checks, requesting transitions only via self.manager.switch_to.
